level_signals: Expected to define and champion vision and roadmap independently
Owns end-to-end product lifecycle for partnerships

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of product management experience, preferably in cloud, SaaS, or developer platforms.
must_haves.qualifier: preferably in cloud, SaaS, or developer platforms

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Proven success in B2B platform integrations and working with partner ecosystems, especially cloud hyperscalers (Microsoft preferred).
Deep understanding of Hyperscaler partnership programs (e.g., IP co-sell, ISV Success, Commercial Marketplace onboarding).
Hands-on experience with cloud infrastructure services and ability to collaborate with engineers on API design, integrations, and infrastructure tradeoffs.
Customer-centric mindset, strong communication and influencing skills, and ability to speak the language of developers, cloud architects, and DevOps professionals.
Strategic thinker with a results-oriented, collaborative approach and problem-solving skills.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Proven success in B2B platform integrations and working with partner ecosystems, especially cloud hyperscalers (Microsoft preferred).

scope.surface: Mistral AI Studio integrations via cloud partner ecosystem (Hyperscalers, OEMs, and others)
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS / cloud marketplace, co-sell motions with hyperscaler partners
core_responsibilities: Drive the product strategy, roadmap, and joint initiatives to grow adoption of Mistral AI Studio through partner ecosystem
Own the end-to-end product lifecycle for partnerships from integration and private preview to general availability and scaling
Conduct market research to understand ecosystem trends, cloud buyer personas, and competitive dynamics
Co-create and execute a joint GTM strategy with field and partner marketing teams to accelerate co-sell motion
Lead packaging and pricing discussions for partner offers
Establish and track KPIs (revenue generation, active users, usage, retention, user satisfaction, CAC, profitability)
Serve as a company and product evangelist through thought leadership, blogging, social media, and industry events

green_flags: Comprehensive benefits package (healthcare, 401K matching, meal stipend, gym reimbursement)
Clear product surface and ownership area (partner ecosystem / Mistral AI Studio integrations)
Hybrid policy with flexibility stated alongside in-office expectation
High-impact, ownership-heavy role with end-to-end lifecycle responsibility
red_flags: Compensation range not disclosed despite competitive market for senior PM roles
Role requires relocation to NYC or existing NYC presence — eliminates remote candidates
At least 3 days/week in-office expectation is a significant commitment
PTO of only 18 days may be below market for a senior IC role
JD does not specify team size or reporting structure, making it difficult to assess org context

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 7+ years requirement and scope of responsibilities)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no mention of direct reports)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and co-sell context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from listed KPIs in JD responsibilities section)
compensation.source set to 'unknown' — only 'competitive salary and equity' stated, no figures given
posting_first_seen and posting_last_updated not available from provided data
ambiguities: The 'preferably in cloud, SaaS, or developer platforms' qualifier on the 7+ years requirement could make it soft, but it is listed unconditionally in the 'Who you are' section — treated as hard with qualifier noted.

Microsoft preference is embedded in a hard-requirement bullet ('Microsoft preferred') — extracted as a separate nice-to-have to reflect the 'preferred' language while retaining the broader hyperscaler experience as a must-have.
Location policy mentions both 'at least 3 days per week' in-office and a 'remote policy' for flexibility — classified as hybrid.
